name: pybedtools (Genomic Interval Operations)

auth.notes: Local library; wraps BEDTools binary (must be installed)

performance.notes: BEDTools C binary backend; fast for genome-scale interval operations

agent_readiness.notes: Python wrapper for BEDTools genome arithmetic. import pybedtools. a = pybedtools.BedTool('peaks.bed'). b = pybedtools.BedTool('genes.bed'). overlaps = a.intersect(b). complement = a.complement(genome='hg38'). closest = a.closest(b). merge = a.merge(d=100). subtract = a.subtract(b). sort = a.sort(). slop = a.slop(b=500, genome='hg38'). Iterate: for feature in a: chrom = feature.chrom; start = feature.start; end = feature.end. DataFrame: a.to_dataframe(). From pandas: pybedtools.BedTool.from_dataframe(df). pybedtools.chromsizes('hg38') → chrom size dict. Temp files auto-cleaned.

gotchas: BEDTools binary must be installed separately (conda install bedtools)
Temp files accumulate if pybedtools.cleanup() not called
BED coordinates are 0-based half-open — different from 1-based GFF/VCF
Large operations create temp files on disk — disk space required
best_when: Genomic interval operations: intersect, merge, subtract, closest, slop
Peak analysis (ChIP-seq, ATAC-seq): overlap with annotation, promoter analysis
When BEDTools command-line is familiar but Python scripting is needed
Integrating genomic coordinates with pandas DataFrames
avoid_when: When PyRanges is preferred for pandas-native interval operations
summary: pybedtools wraps BEDTools for genomic interval operations. BedTool('peaks.bed').intersect(genes).merge(d=100).slop(b=500, genome='hg38'). to_dataframe(). from_dataframe(df). Chrom sizes. Temp file management. MIT by Ryan Dale/NIH. Best for ChIP-seq/ATAC-seq peak analysis and genome arithmetic. BEDTools binary required. 0-based coords. Temp files accumulate — call cleanup(). PyRanges is pandas-native alternative.
